artifact_metadata.intent: Clean final JSON artifact for introducing abstraction governance into the three newPhasePlanProcess v3.2 files.
notes: Part 1 contains only standard RFC-6902 operations using stable object-key JSON Pointer paths.
Part 2 contains non-patch selector instructions for arrays and large text blocks that should be resolved before generating final RFC-6902 operations.
No custom pseudo-ops appear in Part 1.

classification_rules.condition: resource identity must survive rename or move
task kind already exists in approved execution patterns
logic is unique, local, single-use, and non-shared

value.description: Plan-level abstraction control layer for stable references, resolver-based indirection, and shared-core allocation.
features: abstraction_registry with semantic keys and resolver bindings
abstraction_decision_rules for mandatory vs optional abstraction
shared_core_allocation to prevent duplicated shared logic

changes: Added abstraction governance model to the template
Added abstraction registry and resolver contracts
Extended planning semantics to make abstraction a first-class contract

values_to_add: Added abstraction_governance global section for plan-wide abstraction policy and fail-closed enforcement.
Added abstraction_registry global section for declared abstracted resources and resolver bindings.
Added abstraction_decision_rules global section to classify when abstraction is mandatory, optional, or forbidden.
Added shared_core_allocation global section for centralized reusable services and duplication control.
Added resolver_contracts global section for resolver interface contracts and fail-closed lookup semantics.
Cross-module references, shared automation resources, and rename/move-sensitive identities MUST be explicitly classified as DIRECT_ALLOWED or ABSTRACTED.
Every abstraction used by the plan MUST declare a resolver, source of truth, validation rule, and evidence path.
Executable steps MUST declare abstraction_contract when they depend on paths, identities, executors, interfaces, or artifacts that are abstracted.
Forbidden direct references MUST cause fail-closed plan validation.
Before defining file changes, classify each dependency as DIRECT_ALLOWED or ABSTRACTED using abstraction_decision_rules.
Register every abstracted path, identity, executor, interface, and artifact in abstraction_registry before referencing it elsewhere in the plan.
Populate shared_core_allocation for any responsibility reused across multiple CLIs, workstreams, or modules.
For each executable step, define abstraction_contract and verify all abstraction references resolve via resolver_contracts.
Abstraction registry resources must be canonically sorted by abstraction_id before hashing or emitting derived evidence.
Resolver contract entries must be canonically sorted by resolver_id before hashing or emitting derived evidence.

values_to_add: Abstraction governance for stable references, resolver-based indirection, and shared-core allocation
ABSTRACTION AS CONTRACT: Volatile, shared, or identity-sensitive references must be declared, resolved, and validated explicitly
RESOLVER-BOUND INDIRECTION: Abstract references must resolve through declared deterministic resolver contracts
SHARED-CORE FIRST: Reused responsibilities must be centralized unless explicitly exempted

value_to_add.name: Abstraction Governance Layer
value_to_add.role: Declares abstraction policy, abstraction registry entries, resolver contracts, and shared-core allocation for plan generation.

abstraction_registry

values_to_add.purpose: Plan-wide policy defining when abstraction is mandatory, optional, or forbidden and how violations fail closed.
Registry of abstracted resources, semantic keys, resolver bindings, and validation rules.
Classification rules that determine when abstraction is required, allowed, or forbidden.
Declares reusable shared services and forbids uncontrolled duplication of shared responsibilities.
Defines deterministic resolver interfaces, lookup sources of truth, and fail-closed resolution semantics.

values_to_add: Abstraction completeness: all governed dependencies are classified as direct or abstracted
Resolver integrity: all abstraction references resolve through declared deterministic contracts
Shared-core discipline: reusable responsibilities are centralized or explicitly exempted

values_to_upsert.name: Abstraction Coverage
values_to_upsert.purpose: Ensure all cross-boundary, shared, and identity-sensitive dependencies are classified and governed.
values_to_upsert.critical_rule: No governed dependency may remain implicitly direct or unspecified.
values_to_upsert.failure_impact: Fragile or ambiguous dependency model enters execution.

values_to_upsert.name: Resolver Completeness
values_to_upsert.purpose: Ensure every abstraction reference resolves through a declared deterministic resolver contract.
values_to_upsert.critical_rule: No abstraction may exist without exactly one declared resolution path.
values_to_upsert.failure_impact: Execution may depend on unresolved or ambiguous lookup behavior.

values_to_upsert.name: Direct Reference Violation Audit
values_to_upsert.purpose: Reject forbidden literal paths, unresolved executors, and unstable direct references in governed contexts.

values_to_upsert.name: Shared Core Duplication Audit
values_to_upsert.purpose: Detect uncontrolled duplication of reusable responsibilities across CLIs, modules, and workstreams.

values_to_upsert.name: Identity Stability Audit
values_to_upsert.purpose: Ensure rename/move/reordering-sensitive entities do not rely on unstable direct identity mechanisms.

values_to_add.definition: Plan-wide policy that determines when resources must be referenced indirectly through stable semantic or identity-based abstractions.
Registry of abstracted resources, their semantic keys or stable IDs, resolver bindings, and validation rules.
Deterministic interface contract that maps an abstract reference to a concrete resource using a declared source of truth.
Planning decision that centralizes reusable responsibilities into governed shared services rather than duplicating logic across wrappers or workstreams.

updates.text: Version 3.3 adds an abstraction governance layer. Plans must now explicitly classify volatile, shared, cross-module, and identity-sensitive references as direct or abstracted; bind abstracted references to deterministic resolver contracts; and declare shared-core allocation for reusable responsibilities.

bullets: - **Abstraction governance** – Treat abstraction as a planning contract, not an implementation preference. Cross-module references, shared automation resources, and rename/move-sensitive identities must be explicitly classified under `abstraction_governance` and registered in `abstraction_registry`.

- **Resolver-bound indirection** – Every abstracted path, executor, artifact, interface, or identity must resolve through `resolver_contracts`. If no deterministic resolver exists, the plan is incomplete.

- **Shared-core discipline** – Reusable responsibilities must be allocated in `shared_core_allocation` rather than duplicated across CLI wrappers, workstreams, or modules unless a justified exception is documented.

updates.new_section_markdown:
### `abstraction_governance`
Define the plan-wide abstraction policy. Set whether abstraction enforcement is fail-closed, classify default handling for cross-module references, shared automation resources, stable identities, and local stable references, and list forbidden direct-reference types. Reference the governing registries for paths, patterns, executors, and artifacts. Provide a validation command and evidence path.

### `abstraction_registry`
Register every abstracted resource before it is referenced elsewhere in the plan. For each resource, provide `abstraction_id`, `kind`, `semantic_key` or stable identity reference, `physical_binding_source`, `resolver_method`, `direct_reference_allowed`, `stability_reason`, `consumers`, and `validation_rule`. Do not leave abstracted resources implicit.

### `abstraction_decision_rules`
Define the rules that determine when abstraction is mandatory, optional, or forbidden. Include at least cross-module references, rename/move-sensitive identities, known task-pattern matches, and unique local logic. State the conflict policy when multiple rules match.

### `shared_core_allocation`
List reusable responsibilities that must be centralized as shared services. Use this section to prevent duplicated business logic across CLI wrappers, validators, workstreams, or modules. If a reusable responsibility is intentionally duplicated, justify the exception explicitly.

### `resolver_contracts`
Define every resolver used by the plan. Each resolver must declare its input type, output type, source of truth, determinism requirements, and fail-closed behavior. If a plan uses an abstracted dependency but lacks a resolver contract, execution must not proceed.

updates.text: For every system/module/component/execution-packet entry, add `implementation_abstraction` and classify it as `DIRECT`, `ABSTRACTED`, or `HYBRID`. Record abstraction kinds, resolver dependencies, shared-core dependencies, stable-identity dependencies, and justification. `HYBRID` entries require explicit justification.

For each file or artifact reference, record whether it is addressed by direct path, semantic key, stable file ID, or artifact ID. Shared, cross-module, governed, or refactor-sensitive references must not remain implicit direct paths.

Each pattern must declare its abstraction requirements through an abstraction contract. Specify whether path, executor, identity, and artifact abstraction are required and list the resolvers the executor expects. Do not allow a pattern to rely on undeclared direct references when abstraction governance forbids them.

Add mappings for requirement-to-abstraction, module-to-shared-service, component-to-abstraction-id, step-to-resolver, step-to-abstraction-id, and pattern-to-required-abstractions. This makes abstraction auditable rather than implicit.

updates.new_section_markdown:
### Step-level abstraction contract
For every executable step, define an `abstraction_contract` whenever the step depends on abstracted paths, identities, executors, interfaces, or artifacts. The contract must list each abstraction kind, its `abstraction_id`, the resolver used, the semantic key or identity target, forbidden direct usages, and a verification command with an evidence path. A step that uses abstracted resources but omits this contract is incomplete.

updates.find: When no pattern exists, set `pattern_id` to `null` and flag that a new pattern is needed.
updates.replace: When no pattern or required abstraction exists, block implementation planning until the missing pattern or abstraction is created, validated, and registered.

how_to_use: Apply Part 1 directly as RFC-6902 operations where the object-pointer paths exist and are stable.
Process Part 2 against the current file state to resolve arrays and content transforms.
After Part 2 resolution, emit final concrete RFC-6902 operations for those arrays and text replacements.
required_followups: Upgrade plan schema to admit the new top-level sections and nested fields.
Add validators for abstraction governance, abstraction coverage, resolver completeness, direct-reference violations, shared-core duplication, identity stability, and step abstraction contracts.
Regenerate any derived documentation or indexes that depend on required top-level section lists.
